stories.title: EU Commission’s structure leaves Brussels green campaigners dazed and confused
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Ursula von der Leyen’s European Commission structure is proving a headache for green and equality advocates, who say industry has a smoother path to lobbying the institution. With many portfolios split among commissioners, it’s increasingly difficult to reach the right person to try to shape legislation, the advocates told POLITICO’s EU Influence newsletter . stories.summary: Keir Starmer’s programme is fatally limited by the timidity of an election manifesto that shied away from hard arguments Ending 14 years of Conservative rule was supposed to bring an end to dysfunctional government. In the speech that launched his 2024 general election campaign , Sir Keir Starmer said that “a vote for Labour is a vote for stability … a vote to stop the chaos”. Megvárják, hogy lemondanak-e május végéig a közjogi méltóságok, de ha nem, akkor alkotmánymódosítással mozdítják el őket. A három új kormányszóvivő partneri viszonyt ajánlott a sajtónak. Volt kampányfőnöke, Tóth Péter lesz a miniszterelnök nemzetbiztonsági főtanácsadója, Orbán Anita bekérette az orosz nagykövetet, és már az első kormányülésen 17 határozatról tárgyalnak. Mutatjuk a részleteket.

stories.title: Australia politics live: Coalition has gone ‘beyond dogwhistling’ to ‘demonising’, Labor MP says ahead of Taylor’s budget reply speech
stories.summary: Follow today’s news live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Holding Jewish people responsible for Israel a ‘building block’ of racism, inquiry hears There will always be “edge cases”, Dr Dave Rich has told the antisemitism royal commission, where there are good faith disagreements on whether something is antisemitic. Holding Jews collectively responsible for the actions of the state of Israel is the justification provided by terrorists who murder Jews around the world,

stories.title: French authorities lift lockdown on norovirus-hit cruise ship
stories.summary: Asymptomatic people allowed off ship from Wednesday afternoon but those infected ordered to remain in isolation A norovirus-hit cruise ship carrying mainly passengers from the UK and Ireland has had a French lockdown order lifted. All 1,701 people onboard Ambition were prevented from disembarking for more than 24 hours after it docked in Bordeaux on Tuesday, as one person died and dozens became ill with a vomiting virus. Continue reading...

stories.title: UK economy records surprise 0.3% growth in first month of the Iran war
stories.summary: GDP reading ties in with some surveys suggesting UK plc has kept up momentum despite rising fuel costs The UK economy unexpectedly grew during the first full month of the Iran war, according to official figures, suggesting the Middle East conflict has not yet affected growth as much as feared. Figures from the Office for National Statistics (ONS) showed growth of 0.3% in gross domestic product (GDP) in March, down from a revised 0.4% rise in February and 0% growth in January. The ONS had origina

stories.title: Bahamas re-elects Progressive Liberal party’s Philip Davis as prime minister
stories.summary: Davis is the country’s first leader to serve a second consecutive term in nearly 30 years The Bahamas prime minister, Philip Davis, and his ruling Progressive Liberal party (PLP) have been re-elected , making him the country’s first leader to serve a second consecutive term in nearly 30 years. “The Bahamian people have spoken, and I receive their verdict with humility and gratitude,” Davis told Reuters. “This victory is a mandate to keep moving the Bahamas forward, to expand opportunity, strengt

stories.title: Russia targets Ukraine with more than 800 drones in deadly daytime assault
stories.summary: Strikes killed at least six people as Moscow and Kyiv trade long-range attacks after brief ceasefire Europe live – latest updates Russia targeted Ukraine with more than 800 drones in a large-scale daytime assault that killed at least six people on Wednesday, hours after a previous deadly barrage. The strikes came as Kyiv and Moscow traded long-range attacks after a brief ceasefire, and despite the latest suggestion from Donald Trump that the war could soon come to an end. Continue reading...

stories.title: EU proposes end to ‘five tabs, three apps and a prayer’ for cross-border train bookings
stories.summary: New rules would enable single-ticket bookings across multiple rail operators throughout Europe Cross-border train journeys through several European countries are the stuff of many a holidaymaker’s dreams. But the reality of trying to buy the tickets, navigating multiple websites without knowing who can help if a connection is missed, can prove less than relaxing. As one MEP puts it, it can often require “five tabs, three apps and a prayer”. Continue reading...

stories.title: Starmer has ‘full confidence’ in Streeting despite health secretary’s allies saying he is planning to resign – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed, you can read more on this story here: Streeting to resign and challenge Starmer, allies say Libby Brooks is the Guardian’s Scotland correspondent. An odd dispute of interpretation has emerged overnight between the Scottish and UK governments. Yesterday evening a Scottish government spokesperson announced that, during a call between first minister John Swinney and prime minister Kier Starmer, both parties agreed to meet face to face next month to discuss a referendum on i

stories.title: US drops bond requirement for FIFA World Cup ticket holders
stories.summary: Fans from five African World Cup qualifying nations with valid tickets are exempt from paying a hefty bond. But fans from several other countries still face major hurdles to seeing their teams play in the United States.

stories.title: China warns Trump over Taiwan question at high-stakes summit
stories.summary: Chinese state media reported that Xi Jinping raised the question of Taiwan during his meeting with Donald Trump. The pair held talks for about two hours. Follow live.

stories.title: Ukraine court orders arrest of Zelenskyy ally Andriy Yermak
stories.summary: Andriy Yermak resigned in November 2025, after investigators raided his home in a sweeping corruption probe.

stories.title: China goes electric, but can it get off coal?
stories.summary: The world's clean energy superpower also emits the most CO2. But coal-fired power may have peaked as China electrifies its economy with ever more renewables.

stories.title: Iran war: Oil stocks being used up at record pace, IEA warns
stories.summary: The International Energy Agency warned of "rapidly shrinking buffers." Meanwhile, Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi is en route to Delhi for a BRICS gathering which is set to be dominated by the conflict.

stories.title: Indians growing anxious as Modi asks them to cut on fuel
stories.summary: With energy prices rising, Prime Minister Narendra Modi has urged Indians to embrace voluntary austerity to protect the economy from the fallout of the war in Iran. But residents say they are already at their limit.

stories.title: Can South Africa's President Ramaphosa avoid impeachment again?
stories.summary: Ramaphosa sidestepped an impeachment inquiry in 2022 when his ANC-dominated parliament blocked it. Now that South Africa's top court has revived the case, can he escape with a parliament accomodating former adversaries?

stories.title: Germany's shortfall in medium-range defense capabilities
stories.summary: The US has announced that it will not be deploying Tomahawk cruise missiles in Germany. Now the German government is looking for alternatives. Could drones be part of the solution?

stories.title: Russia unleashes fatal barrage of drones at Ukraine
stories.summary: At least six people died in Ukraine after Russia launched a daytime attack of 800 drones of drones as far as western regions bordering NATO. Zelenskyy said it was one of the longest and largest attacks of the war.

stories.title: Passengers allowed to leave norovirus-hit cruise ship
stories.summary: Passengers on the ship showing no symptoms are allowed to leave, authorities say, after 49 people fell ill from gastrointestinal sickness.

stories.title: Trump arrives in Bejing for crucial summit, welcomed by Vice President Han
stories.summary: The US president said he will be talking to his Chinese counterpart about trade “more than anything else.”

stories.title: 250,000 pilgrims gather at Fatima shrine in Portugal for annual candlelight Mass
stories.summary: Hundreds of thousands of Catholic pilgrims gathered at Portugal’s Fatima shrine for the annual Mass of the Blessing of the Candles honouring the Virgin Mary.

stories.title: Hezbollah support endures in south Lebanon as ceasefire fails to stop war with Israel
stories.summary: Despite continuing Israeli attacks and occupation, many people in the south still believe the armed group is the only force capable of defending them.

stories.title: Court overturns Alex Murdaugh's murder convictions and orders new trial
stories.summary: The court has ordered a new trial over the June 2021 killings of Paul and Maggie Murdaugh.

stories.title: Xi warns Trump that Taiwan issue could lead to 'conflict' at bilateral talks in Beijing
stories.summary: Taiwan's foreign ministry said that China is the "sole risk" to regional peace and stability, after Xi warned Trump that the issue of Taiwan could lead to conflict.

stories.title: Russian attack on Kyiv continues overnight, killing at least one
stories.summary: The drone and missile attack comes hours after a rare daytime attack on the Ukrainian capital, which had killed at least six people.

stories.title: 'They shot my neighbour in the head' - the lakeside city traumatised by war
stories.summary: Rebel fighters and Rwandan troops are accused of committing atrocities after capturing the DR Congo city of Uvira in December.

stories.title: Watch: Key moments from Trump’s first day in Beijing
stories.summary: China welcomed US President Donald Trump to Beijing on Thursday for a closely watched summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping, as the two powers sought to stabilise relations strained by trade disputes, tensions over Taiwan and the ongoing conflict involving Iran.

stories.title: Ukraine: Russian drone and missile barrage slams Kyiv, killing at least one
stories.summary: Ukraine’s capital Kyiv was struck by a large-scale Russian drone and missile attack early on Thursday, leaving at least one person dead and 31 injured, according to local officials.

stories.title: Pomp and pageantry: Key moments from day one of Trump's visit to China
stories.summary: China welcomed US President Donald Trump with cheering children and a troop parade on Thursday, before a nearly two-hour long meeting with Xi-Jinping.

stories.title: Iran preparing for renewed war as military assets remain largely intact, reports warn
stories.summary: Iran is likely preparing for renewed conflict with the US and Israel, Washington-based ISW think tank assessed, citing military drills and asset repositioning after reports emerged that Tehran remained in possession of significant missile stockpiles.

stories.title: Starmer leadership crisis: Streeting readies bid to oust UK PM — live updates
stories.summary: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer is braced for an imminent challenge to his position with Health Secretary Wes Streeting expected to launch a bid for the leadership of the ruling Labour Party Thursday. It comes as former Deputy Prime Minister Angela Rayner, another potential rival, said she has been cleared of wrongdoing in a tax probe — overcoming a major hurdle to her own potential challenge for the top job. Starmer is still reeling from dire election results in England, Wales and Scotland las

stories.title: Ghana to evacuate 300 from South Africa over anti-immigrant protests
stories.summary: The foreign minister says the "distressed" Ghanaians registered with the embassy in Pretoria to be evacuated.

stories.title: US man accused of killing rock singer in 1993 arrested after decades on the run
stories.summary: Richard Werstine, wanted in connection with killing of Cold as Life vocalist Rodney Barger, was arrested in Panama A suspect accused of murdering his rock singer friend in 1993 has been apprehended in Central America after spending more than 30 years successfully evading authorities. According to the US Marshals Service, Richard Werstine, who was wanted in connection with the killing of Cold as Life vocalist Rodney Barger, was arrested in Panama. Continue reading...

stories.title: Les Simpson: return of Québécois show spares viewers from ‘European French’
stories.summary: Beloved animated series will return for 36th season in the fall after telecoms giant Bell Media reaches deal with Disney Fans of Les Simpson have a message for anyone who doubted the future of the beloved and long-running Québécois version of the animated satirical show: Mange de la crotte. Les Simpson will return for its 36th season in the fall after telecoms giant Bell Media said it had reached an agreement with Disney for the rights to air and dub the show. The deal caps nearly a year of unce

stories.title: Milka maker milked shoppers over size of chocolate bars, German court rules
stories.summary: Brand owner Mondelēz was accused of reducing weight of Alpine Milk bar from 100g to 90g without significantly altering the packaging Many chocolate lovers consider shrinkflation a serious crime – and they have been vindicated after a German court ruled that the makers of Milka cheated consumers by cutting the bar’s size, while keeping the wrapper the same. The three-week case in a regional court was brought by Hamburg’s consumer protection office. It accused the chocolate brand’s US owner Mondel

stories.title: Benjamin Netanyahu says he made secret trip to UAE at height of Iran war
stories.summary: Emirates’ foreign ministry rejects claims that Netanyahu visited the country describing them as ‘baseless’ Benjamin Netanyahu has claimed he made a secret trip to the United Arab Emirates at the height of the Iran war to meet the president, Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan. “This visit has led to a historic breakthrough in relations between Israel and the UAE,” the Israeli prime minister’s office said on Wednesday night. Continue reading...

stories.title: Family of British couple jailed in Iran say they have lost contact
stories.summary: Lindsay and Craig Foreman were given 10-year sentences after entering the country on a motorcycling trip The “terrified” family of a British couple jailed for 10 years in Iran on spying charges have said they have lost all contact with them. Lindsay and Craig Foreman, both 53, were arrested in January 2025 while travelling through Iran during an around-the-world trip by motorcycle. Continue reading...

stories.title: China’s new rules give the West a new headache
stories.summary: Beijing now has strong new powers to punish firms for pulling manufacturing out of China. Multinationals are increasingly caught in a web of coercion and complex rules as they try to operate across the US, EU and China.

stories.title: Ceasefire in sight? What's next for Russia's war in Ukraine
stories.summary: Four years since Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine, is the end finally in sight? DW spoke to US political experts about why they think US home elections could bring about a ceasefire.

stories.title: Cem Özdemir, first German state premier with Turkish roots
stories.summary: Cem Özdemir is the first leader of a German federal state to have Turkish roots. His Green Party won elections in the wealthy southwestern region of Baden-Württemberg.

stories.title: Israeli-Palestinian conflict: Allegations of sexual abuse
stories.summary: The issue of systemic sexual abuses as part of the Israeli-Palestinian conflict is back in the spotlight following new testimonies. Both Israel and Hamas deny the claims.

stories.title: Tigray tensions raise fears of Ethiopia-Eritrea war
stories.summary: The TPLF is reasserting control in Tigray after electing Debretsion Gebremichael. Rising tensions with Ethiopia's federal government are fueling fears of renewed conflict in the Horn of Africa.

stories.title: Trump's Federal Reserve chair nominee confirmed by Senate
stories.summary: Kevin Warsh is set to replace outgoing Fed chair Jerome Powell, who has been a frequent target of President Trump's criticism.

stories.title: Russian cargo ship sunk off Spain carried nuclear reactors for North Korea, probe reveals

stories.title: Cannes Film Festival 2026: Fans lock down red carpet spots ahead of opening
stories.summary: Film fans gather overnight at Cannes, using stepladders and chains to secure red carpet spots as they wait for a glimpse of opening night stars.

stories.title: Australian property developer says Trump hotel plan scrapped due to 'toxic' brand

stories.title: Gaza sisters win prize for turning rubble into reusable bricks
stories.summary: Displaced teenagers Farah and Tala wanted to 'turn destruction into something useful'.

stories.title: Climbers clear path to Everest summit past giant chunk of ice
stories.summary: Nearly 1,000 climbers will attempt to scale the peak in coming weeks and this has raised safety concerns.

stories.title: What to know as Trump visits Xi in China
stories.summary: Trump has landed in Beijing for the first visit by a US leader since his own in 2017.

stories.title: Watch: UK Parliament revives centuries-old ritual before King’s Speech
stories.summary: Black Rod was symbolically denied entry to the House of Commons during the ceremonial opening of the UK Parliament ahead of King Charles III’s speech.

stories.title: Dozens of dogs rescued and suspect arrested in Uganda after BBC investigation

stories.title: Woman isolating on British island in South Pacific after hantavirus contact
stories.summary: The woman who was on a virus-hit ship shows "no signs of illness", the government of Pitcairn Islands, a British Overseas Territory, says.

stories.title: Thousands of Waymos recalled after robotaxi swept into a creek
stories.summary: The voluntary recall follows an incident on 20 April where an empty Waymo car entered a flooded road in San Antonio, Texas.

stories.title: Trade, Iran and Taiwan on the agenda as Trump arrives in China for high-stakes talks with Xi
stories.summary: The US president says his first request to Xi would be to "open up" China, while Beijing is expected to press Trump on the status of Taiwan.

stories.title: Syria needs 'comprehensive security agreement' with Israel, foreign minister tells Euronews
stories.summary: Al-Shaibani calls for a return to the 1974 security agreement between Israel and Syria and demands the withdrawal from territories seized. Remarks coincide with high-level political dialogue between Damascus and Brussels.

stories.title: Europe watches Beijing summit from the sidelines and fears the worst
stories.summary: The highly anticipated summit between US President Trump and his Chinese host Xi Jinping has begun - and Europe is watching from a distance. Yet, whatever the outcome is, there is little Brussels can be optimistic about.

stories.summary: Britain’s Catherine, Princess of Wales visited the Italian city of Reggio Emilia during her first official overseas trip since announcing her cancer remission.

stories.summary: Közérdekűadat-igényléssel kikértük, hogy a kulturális minisztérium mennyi közpénzt adott a fideszes hegedűsnek az elmúlt években. Alig négy év alatt az NKA éves költségvetésének harmada landolt Mágánál és köreinél, még egy frissen alapított céget is megdobtak félmilliárddal.

stories.title: EU invites Taliban members to discuss Afghan migrant returns
stories.summary: The European Commission has invited officials from Afghanistan's Taliban to discuss the returns of certain migrants. It's controversial on humanitarian grounds, and because Brussels doesn't recognize Taliban authority.

stories.title: A weakened Trump to test China's appetite for deal-making
stories.summary: A quagmire in Iran, sky-high oil prices and shrinking support at home: when US President Donald Trump belatedly travels to China this week, his position has weakened. Still, he is hoping for any deal. Nervous Europeans are watching.

stories.title: Cannes is a beautiful, gruelling circus. I wouldn’t quit it for anything | Agnès Poirier
stories.summary: The festival is a celebration of cinema and a frantic trade show all at once. After 25 years, I can’t help but go back Nothing prepares you for the shock that is the Cannes film festival: the adrenaline, the fatigue, the elation and the emotion, but also the hunger, the anger, the magic and the ridicule. stories.title: UK: Keir Starmer tells Cabinet he will continue in place, as four junior ministers quit
stories.summary: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has met with his Cabinet amid continued pressure after election losses. A set piece speech yesterday failed to calm the waters, and four junior ministers resigned on Tuesday.

stories.title: Hotter 2026 and El Nino could trigger extreme fires
stories.summary: More than 150 million hectares — over twice the size of Texas — burned globally in the first months of 2026. With a high chance of a supercharged El Nino, the second half of the year could be even worse.

stories.title: Why men are less worried than women about climate change
stories.summary: From petro-masculinity to electric trucks: why men disengage from climate action and what might bring them back.

stories.title: Satellites add a new layer to global poverty data
stories.summary: A new study using satellite data finds that 58% of the global population has been assigned to the wrong tier in the UN's Human Development Index. That can have real-world consequences for how aid reaches people.

stories.summary: Macron joined marathon legend Eliud Kipchoge for an early morning run in Nairobi on day three of his Africa tour, later thanking the two-time Olympic champion for his "patience."

stories.title: At least nine killed after bomb rigged to rickshaw explodes at north Pakistan bazaar
stories.summary: The Pakistani Taliban and other militant groups have grown more emboldened since the Afghan Taliban returned to power in Kabul in 2021.

stories.title: Iran threatens weapons-grade uranium enrichment as peace talks falter
stories.summary: Tehran threatened to enrich uranium to weapons-grade if attacked again, as Trump said the ceasefire had a "one percent chance" of surviving after rejecting Tehran's proposal.

stories.title: Qatar warns Iran against weaponising Hormuz to 'blackmail' Gulf countries
stories.summary: Qatar warned Tehran on Tuesday against using the Strait of Hormuz as a "weapon" to hold Gulf states to ransom, as Doha and Ankara backed ongoing mediation efforts to end the Iran war and restore access to the vital waterway.

stories.title: Chinese people divided ahead of Trump-Xi summit in Beijing
stories.summary: Residents in Beijing shared mixed opinions ahead of the upcoming meeting between US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping, with some hoping for improved ties and others expressing skepticism.

stories.title: Peacocks take over northern Italian beach town
stories.summary: More than 100 peacocks now roam Punta Marina in northern Italy, leaving residents searching for humane ways to manage the birds.

stories.title: Kazakhstan launches driverless Light Rail Transit system after 10 years of delays
stories.summary: After a decade of construction delays, Astana has launched its Light Rail Transit (LRT) system, featuring driverless overground trains serving 18 stations across the city.

stories.title: SOCAR acquisition of Italy’s Italiana Petroli finalised after EU clearance
stories.summary: SOCAR has completed its acquisition of Italiana Petroli after securing all regulatory approvals, marking a major expansion of its downstream presence in Italy and strengthening its position in Europe’s energy market.

stories.title: Palestinians gather to mark 78th anniversary of the Nakba and call for 'right of return'
stories.summary: Palestinians in the occupied West Bank and the Gaza Strip marked the 78th anniversary of the Nakba, a defining moment in Palestinian history dating back to 1948, when hundreds of thousands of people were forced to flee their homes, villages and land, becoming refugees.

stories.title: Lebanon says two paramedics among 13 killed in Israeli strikes
stories.summary: Lebanon's health ministry says the paramedics were carrying out a rescue mission in Nabatieh in response to an earlier attack that killed one person.

stories.title: Australia has some of the world's costliest homes. Will scrapping tax breaks help?
stories.summary: The government hopes reforms will help young people enter the market, while critics say it will stifle supply.

stories.title: A decade on, Trump returns to a stronger and more assertive China
stories.summary: Beijing is arguably the most powerful competitor the US has confronted in its history, one analyst says.

stories.title: Air India crisis deepens ahead of final Ahmedabad crash report
stories.summary: Air India faces a leadership vacuum and mounting financial losses as it struggles to recover from the crash.

stories.title: Jason Collins, NBA's first openly gay player, dies aged 47
stories.summary: Collins shared late last year that he had been diagnosed with an aggressive form of brain cancer.

stories.title: Trump's 'Golden Dome' will cost $1.2tn and might not stop all-out missile attack
stories.summary: That pricetag is nearly seven times higher than Trump's initial estimate, found an independent budget office.

stories.title: Pentagon says US has spent $29 billion on Iran war so far, amid concerns over weapons stockpiles
stories.summary: Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth came under intense questioning by Democratic and Republican lawmakers over the cost of war, Trump's end game and diminishing weapons stockpiles.

stories.title: China’s Unitree unveils transformable humanoid ‘mecha’ robot with cockpit
stories.summary: Chinese robotics company Unitree Robotics unveiled a transformable humanoid “mecha” robot featuring a cockpit for a human pilot.

stories.title: Trump arrives in Beijing for high-stakes summit with Xi
stories.summary: This week's trip, the first since Trump visited Beijing in 2017, will involve highly anticipated talks with Xi on Thursday and Friday.

stories.title: Barcelona star Lamine Yamal waves Palestinian flag during La Liga celebrations
stories.summary: Footage released on May 12 showed Yamal waving a Palestinian flag during FC Barcelona’s La Liga title parade celebrating the club’s 29th league title. In a news conference, Barcelona coach Hansi Flick said he did not like such gestures, but added that Yamal was old enough to make his own decisions.

stories.title: EU preparing to offer key concession to UK in new post-Brexit agricultural deal
stories.summary: Exclusive: Britain expected to be allowed to keep ban on live animal exports, sources say, in fillip for Keir Starmer UK politics live – latest updates Brussels is preparing to offer Keir Starmer a key concession in talks over an agricultural deal, giving the beleaguered prime minister an important victory in his efforts to move closer to the EU. European officials have conceded that the UK can keep its ban on live animal exports as part of any joint deal on food and agricultural products, accor

stories.title: Germany news: 1-in-6 retailers fear for future
stories.summary: About one-in-12 German businesses worry about survival, with twice as many retailers jittery. Meanwhile in Stuttgart, five pro-Palestinian activists face trial over attacking an Israeli defense company site. Follow DW.

stories.title: Russia launches strikes at Ukraine, hours after expiration of 72-hour truce
stories.summary: On Sunday, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Ukraine would retaliate if Russia decided "to return to a full-scale war."

stories.title: Yes, Europe’s leaders are finally standing up to Trump. Here’s why | Mujtaba Rahman
stories.summary: Friedrich Merz’s criticism of the US president was not a solo run. It was born of the realisation that US leverage has slipped Friedrich Merz’s criticism of Donald Trump last month reflected more than a moment of personal candour or a split between Berlin and the White House. It pointed to a broader shift under way among European leaders. Increasingly they are willing to publicly confront the Trump administration on issues ranging from Iran to Ukraine and European sovereignty. The Trump administ

stories.title: Gaborone gold rush: how Botswana rose to the top of men’s sprinting
stories.summary: Country with a population of just 2.5m credits investment in young athletes for its rise but this progress is under threat It was a fairytale ending to the World Athletics Relays in Gaborone. In the final strait, Collen Kebinatshipi surged past South Africa’s Zakithi Nene to win the men’s 4x400m relay for Botswana. The home crowd, a sea of light blue, went wild. “It means so many things to us,” Letsile Tebogo, 22, the reigning 200m Olympic champion, who ran the second leg, told reporters afterwa

stories.title: Internal displacements caused by violence or conflict at record high in 2025
stories.summary: The 32.3m surpasses those caused by disasters for the first time, as 82.2m people displaced in total around world The number of internal displacements triggered by conflict or violence around the world reached a record high in 2025, surpassing the number of disaster-driven internal displacements for the first time. A report published by the Internal Displacement Monitoring Centre (IDMC) shows that by the end of 2025 there were 32.3m conflict-driven internal displacements. That is 60% higher than

stories.title: Somali piracy disrupts global shipping and trade routes
stories.summary: As ships reroute around Africa to avoid Middle East conflict zones, piracy off Somalia is back. The resurgence is driving up insurance, transit times and security costs, putting new pressure on global supply chains.

stories.title: Endemic, epidemic, pandemic: What's the difference?
stories.summary: The Andes hantavirus outbreak shows how easily disease labels are misunderstood — and why they describe patterns of spread, not how dangerous a virus is.

stories.title: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer 'not walking away' after local election mauling
stories.summary: Keir Starmer has said he takes "responsibility" for Labour's poor election performance, but also for putting matters right again. "I know I have my doubters and I know I need to prove them wrong, and I will," he said.

stories.title: Hantavirus: How it differs from COVID
stories.summary: In 2018-2019, the Andes virus hantavirus spread person-to-person in Argentina. Eleven people died — but it was stopped. Analysis of that outbreak shows the current one can also be stopped.

stories.title: Architecture of remembrance and hope: Daniel Libeskind at 80
stories.summary: Famous for designing the Jewish Museum Berlin and the Ground Zero project in New York, the architect has created dozens of memorable buildings worldwide.

stories.title: Hantavirus: How is the outbreak being contained?
stories.summary: Passengers evacuated from the MV Hondius cruise ship are returning to their home countries, some with symptoms, many without. What happens to these people and what is being done to contain the spread?

stories.title: Ukrainian children held in Russia: militarized, 'reeducated'
stories.summary: A Ukrainian initiative to repatriate abducted Ukrainian children reports that at least 20,000 girls and boys are being held by Russian families and authorities. Can these children ever find their way home?

stories.title: EU negotiators agree new rules on vital medicines
stories.summary: A new agreement aims to tackle the drug shortages that have plagued the bloc. Among other things, it would make it easier to use public funds to support the production of essential medicines.

stories.title: What now for the role of high representative in Bosnia?
stories.summary: Christian Schmidt's departure as high representative in Bosnia and Herzegovina doesn't look like a routine diplomatic handover. It seems to indicate that international powers want something different from the role.

stories.title: Hijab-wearing German beauty contestant takes on the AfD
stories.summary: As a Miss Germany contestant, Büsra Sayed wore a hijab on stage. This had never happened before — and the far-right AfD didn't like it. Her response went viral.

stories.title: Trump's China visit set to test fragile truce
stories.summary: The first US presidential visit to China in almost 10 years will test a fragile tariff truce.

stories.title: Watch: Soldier on parachuting to remote island to bring Hantavirus aid
stories.summary: A specialist team helped supply critical medical support to Tristan da Cunha in the South Atlantic.
